name: Fruit-Filled Puffs
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F. Spray a large baking sheet with nonstick cooking spray; set aside.
2. In a medium saucepan, combine 1 cup water, the oil, and vanilla. Bring to a boil. Stir in matzoh cake meal and salt; continue cooking, stirring constantly, about 1 minute, until mixture no longer sticks to side of saucepan. Remove from heat and stir in eggs, one at a time, stirring thoroughly after each addition.
3. Drop dough by 12 rounded tablespoonfuls, about 2 inches apart, onto prepared baking sheet. Bake for 10 minutes. Reduce heat to 400°F and bake an additional 10-15 minutes, until browned and puffed. Cool completely on rack, then slice in half horizontally.
4. To fill, fit a pastry bag with a 1/4-inch diameter star tip. Fill the bag with whipped topping. Divide fruit evenly among bottom halves of puffs. Pipe or spoon about 2 tablespoons topping over each filled half. Place tops on puffs and drizzle with 1/2 teaspoon chocolate syrup.
Each serving provides: 1 FA, 1/2 P, 1/2 B, 1/2 FR, 25 C.
Per serving: 178 cal, 5 g pro, 9 g fat, 20 g car, 129 mg sod, 106 mg chol.
